response.content: List of Michelin 3-star restaurants - Wikipedia Jump to content From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia Michelin stars are a rating system used by the red Michelin Guide to grade restaurants on their quality. The guide was originally developed in 1900 to show French drivers where local amenities such as restaurants and mechanics were. The rating system was first introduced in 1926 as a single star, with the second and third stars introduced in 1933. [ 1 ] According to the Guide, one star signifies "a very good restaurant", two stars are "excellent cooking that is worth a detour", and three stars mean "exceptional cuisine that is worth a special journey". [ 2 ] The listing of starred restaurants is updated once per year. Summary [ edit ] The 2025 Michelin Guides list 156 restaurants with 3 Michelin stars: [ 3 ] Countries/Regions by number of 3 Michelin Stars restaurants: Rank Country/Region Number 1 § France (incl. response.content: Here Are All the Michelin Starred Restaurants in the San Francisco Bay Area | Eater SF Skip to main content App Icon Get our App App Icon App The homepage Eater Eater logo Navigation Drawer close Close Visit another Eater city site Atlanta Austin Boston Carolinas Chicago Dallas Denver Detroit Houston Las Vegas Los Angeles Miami Nashville New Orleans New York Philadelphia Phoenix Portland, OR San Diego San Francisco Seattle Twin Cities Washington, D.C. Eater.com Facebook Twitter Instagram Youtube RSS Enclos in Sonoma Patricia Chang Dining Out in SF All the Michelin Star Restaurants in the Bay Area, Mapped Discover the stars closest to you, or strategize your driving and dining itinerary by Dianne de Guzman Updated Dec 18, 2025, 10:33 PM UTC Facebook Link Dianne de Guzman is the regional editor for Eater’s Northern California/Pacific Northwest sites, writing about restaurant and bar trends, upcoming openings, and pop-ups for the San Francisco Bay Area, Portland, and Seattle. The new year is upon us, and if you’re the type for resolutions or bucket lists involving restaurants, consider this map your signal to add some Michelin-starred pep to your meals. Michelin updated its California guide again in June 2025, doling out a fresh round of stars at a ceremony held at the Safe Credit Union Performing Arts Center in Sacramento. Two new restaurants earned their place on the list, with Sun Moon Studio in Oakland earning a one-star designation, and Sonoma’s Enclos leaping to two stars. With the latest ceremony, the San Francisco Bay Area has stars awarded to 48 restaurants, with the highest concentration of three-star restaurants, and ten Green Star designations for “outstanding eco-friendly commitments.” (Notably, San Francisco’s one-Michelin-starred restaurant the Shota is temporarily closed as of March 2025, but retains its star.)
